description: Local Python CLI worker for Czech government and tax filing automation (DPH + Kontrolní hlášení + DPPO/DPFO). Produces filing artefacts (XML/PDF) and accountant reports from bank statements, manual CSV/Excel, and invoice documents. Flat-file persistence, no AWS, no portal login in v1.
last_session_summary: Session 1: Task 0.1 complete. Read input/requirements.txt, explored playbook examples, gathered gap-filling requirements from user (jurisdiction, language, trigger, deliverables, processes, data sources, persistence, secrets). Discovered mid-session that resources/ contains invoice folders — added to .gitignore and flagged as additional data source.

phase_0_planning.name: Planning & Setup
phase_0_planning.description: Create implementation spec, get approval, generate tasks

tasks.name: Read input materials and explore examples
tasks.verify: session_notes.md contains per-file summary of EVERY document in input/ with extracted requirements, constraints, and decisions
tasks.verify_result: PASSED - session_notes.md contains per-file breakdown of input/requirements.txt plus gap-filling answers from user covering jurisdiction, language, trigger, deliverables, processes, data sources, persistence, secrets.
tasks.notes: CRITICAL: Read EVERY file in input/ folder thoroughly. For each document: (1) identify its type and purpose, (2) extract ALL requirements, constraints, decisions, and domain terms, (3) note any external URLs that need fetching via WebFetch. Write a per-file breakdown in session_notes.md showing what each file contains and how it will inform the spec. requirements.md (or equivalent) is the PRIMARY document - every requirement in it must be addressed. Do NOT skim or summarize loosely - extract specifics. Then explore playbook examples to find best match. SESSION 1 NOTE: input/ contained only requirements.txt (2 lines). Requirements were too sparse, so user answered 8 gap questions in-session — those answers recorded in session_notes.md are authoritative requirements alongside requirements.txt. Mid-session, resources/ (invoice folders) appeared on disk — added to .gitignore and noted as additional data source beyond what was covered in gap Q6. A follow-up gap task 0.1a is added to confirm invoice handling before spec drafting.

tasks.name: Confirm invoice data-source handling
tasks.added_reason: resources/Přijaté Faktury/ and resources/Vydané faktury/ appeared during session 1 after gap Q6 was already answered. Need to confirm: (1) are invoices an in-scope data source for v1, (2) what file formats (PDF, ISDOC, XML, images)?, (3) how do invoices relate to VAT calculation vs. just being archived? — before drafting spec in 0.3.
tasks.verify: session_notes.md records invoice-handling decision (in scope / out of scope / deferred) with formats and role

tasks.name: Select playbook template and document rationale
Draft IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md sections 1-3
tasks.verify: IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md exists with Overview, Architecture, Technical Specs
tasks.notes: Project Overview, Architecture, Technical Specifications. CRITICAL: Only include CONFIRMED items. Speculative content (candidate instances, unverified schemas) belongs in tasks, not spec. 100 lines confirmed > 600 lines speculative. TRACEABILITY: Every section must be grounded in input materials from task 0.1. Reference which input documents informed each decision. If input/ contains requirements.md, it is the PRIMARY source - the spec must address every requirement in it. Do not write from general knowledge when input materials provide specifics.

tasks.name: Draft IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md sections 4-7
tasks.notes: Phases, AWS Resources, Repository Structure, Success Criteria. For unknown domains, use exploration-first: Phase 1 = 'Setup & Exploration' to discover actual structure before defining detailed phases. TRACEABILITY: Each phase must trace back to requirements from input materials. Add a References section listing ALL input documents and how each influenced the spec. Every file in input/ must be accounted for.

tasks.name: Present spec to user and get approval
tasks.notes: BLOCKING: Do not proceed until user says 'approved' or equivalent

tasks.name: Create project CLAUDE.md
tasks.notes: Create CLAUDE.md from template, fill in project-specific values

tasks.name: Generate Phase 1 tasks
tasks.verify: tasks/phase_1_*.md exists, progress.json has Phase 1 tasks

tasks.name: Generate remaining phase tasks
tasks.verify: All phase task files exist, progress.json complete
tasks.notes: May span multiple sessions if many phases

tasks.name: Remove examples repo and commit Phase 0 completion
tasks.verify: syndicate-playbooks-examples/ removed, git log shows Phase 0 complete commit
tasks.notes: Examples no longer needed. Remove dir, update .gitignore, commit.

orchestration.notes: Must be git repo from start. Commit after each task. No remote configured yet.
